propositions: Plan-review sidecar RETAIN_PER_TICKET is raised from 10 to 50 while keeping newest-first prune ordering.
Code-review sidecar currently lacks prune() and RETAIN_PER_TICKET, so its REVIEW_RESULT events grow unbounded.
Code-review sidecar gains a prune() mirroring plan-review (list events by filename timestamp, git rm all but newest keep, commit --no-verify, best-effort exception-swallowing).
The retention bound is a single shared constant imported by code_review from plan_review, with no duplicated literal.
code_review.sidecar.emit() calls prune after append_event, matching plan-review's emit-then-prune sequence.
After 50+ reviews on one ticket, both paths retain exactly 50 newest REVIEW_RESULT sidecars and prune older ones on the next write.
